body.conclusion: Ace Alliance finds the proposed "Cardano at TOKEN2049 Singapore 2026: Baseline ‘Platinum' Sponsorship Proposal" Treasury Withdrawals Governance Action Constitutional.
body.counterargumentDiscussion:
A reviewer might argue that the 3 percent Compliance and Governance allocation (USD 22,421) is materially smaller than the audit allocation in the bundled predecessor, and that this raises a question under Article II.7.4. The Constitution requires that ada be allocated from the withdrawal to cover periodic independent audits and the implementation of oversight metrics; it does not prescribe a minimum percentage or dollar figure. The combination of a dedicated 3 percent line item, the public Intersect dashboard for on-chain smart-contract state and KPI tracking, and the committed 60-day post-event report satisfies the requirement on its face.

A reviewer might further argue that EMURGO is both the operational facilitator and the recipient of pass-through funds, and that this dual role complicates the administrator-versus-recipient separation contemplated by Article II.7. The proposal addresses this by naming Intersect, not EMURGO, as the administrator, with EMURGO as the recipient under a PSSC. This is the same pattern used in prior TWGAs that Ace Alliance has found Constitutional, and we treat it consistently.

A reviewer might further argue that the booth-and-event "pass-through" framing leaves substantial discretion to EMURGO over vendor selection and on-the-ground execution. The Constitution does not require granular vendor disclosure at the proposal stage; it requires a stated purpose, period, costs, refund terms, audit allocation, named administrator, and custody safeguards. Those elements are present, and post-event accountability is enforced through the milestone structure and the public dashboard.
body.precedentDiscussion:
This proposal is one of three TWGAs submitted in 2026-05 following the expiration of the bundled "Cardano Summit 2026 and TOKEN2049 Singapore" TWGA. The bundled action was reviewed by Ace Alliance and found Constitutional (see `202604/summit_2026/rationale.md`), but did not meet the DRep approval threshold and expired on-chain. The proposers responded to DRep feedback on budget scale by splitting the original ask into three independent governance actions, of which this Baseline ‘Platinum' TOKEN2049 sponsorship is one. The constitutional framework Ace Alliance applied to the bundled predecessor remains the applicable framework for each of the three split actions. We re-apply that framework here against the downscaled scope.

Under Article II.7.3, we continue to apply the framework first articulated in `202603/amaru_treasury_withdrawal_2026/rationale.md` and reaffirmed in `202603/cardano_x_draper_dragon/rationale.md`, treating the 350M ada NCL for epochs 613 to 713 as definitively set and active for the purposes of evaluating TWGAs that operate within its remaining balance.

The smart-contract custody pattern (Intersect TRSC plus PSSC, auto-abstain delegation, no SPO, Sundae Labs audited contracts, multi-signature Oversight Committee) is the same pattern accepted as Article II.7.5 and II.7.6 compliant across `202603/amaru_treasury_withdrawal_2026`, `202603/cardano_x_draper_dragon`, and `202604/summit_2026`. We apply the same finding here.
body.rationaleStatement:
This Treasury Withdrawals Governance Action (TWGA) requests 3,303,750 ada (approximately USD 792,900 at an assumed rate of USD 0.24 per ada) to fund a ‘Platinum' tier sponsorship at TOKEN2049 Singapore on October 7 to 8, 2026. EMURGO is named as the operational facilitator and direct recipient of pass-through funds, with Intersect designated as the administrator operating on behalf of the Cardano Development Holdings (CDH). The withdrawal flows to Intersect's 2025 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract (TRSC), thereafter to a Project-Specific Smart Contract (PSSC) for EMURGO, with a two-milestone disbursement structure. Our review concerns the constitutionality of the action as posted, not the merits of the underlying sponsorship strategy or the negotiated commercial terms.

Under Article II.6, governance actions must use a standardized format, include an immutable URL and hash, and provide sufficient rationale. This TWGA satisfies these requirements.

**Article II.6.1 (Metadata Format).** The metadata is served from immutable IPFS (`ipfs://bafkreibmdzs4o5samwbjct7j6ufjbvqfyqj7yudgrklnb2yi3yznle3yae`) with a matching anchor hash referenced on-chain (`0a6073c78fa07d0f8cd0db57a47b8e0e4569b906c80051a77afb28bac23ff119`), and conforms to CIP-0108. Content-addressed IPFS storage forecloses post-submission alteration.

**Article II.6.2 (Metadata Structure).** The metadata contains a title, abstract, motivation, rationale, an explicit constitutional self-assessment, a budget breakdown, key performance indicators, and risk and mitigation discussion.

Under Article II.7, TWGAs must meet six specific requirements. This TWGA satisfies all of them.

**Article II.7.1 (Terms of the Withdrawal).** Purpose is stated (securing a ‘Platinum' Sponsorship at TOKEN2049 Singapore to deliver a booth, builder substage, distributed conference tickets, and associated branding). The delivery period is specified (October 7 to 8, 2026, with a post-event report committed within 60 days). Relevant costs are itemized across four lines (Platinum sponsorship package USD 399,900, booth build and logistics USD 250,000, management and community support at 15 percent USD 97,485, compliance and governance at 3 percent USD 22,421) plus Intersect's 3 percent administrative fee of USD 23,094, totaling USD 792,900. Refund circumstances are enumerated in three cases: cancellation of TOKEN2049 before vendor payments are due, surplus from unspent ada returned within six months, and surplus arising from ada price appreciation returned within six months.

**Article II.7.2 (Prior Funding Disclosure).** EMURGO discloses two prior 2025 Treasury receipts: the "Unveiling the First Unified Global Events Marketing Strategy for Cardano" TWGA (still in delivery for activities scheduled in 2026) and the "Cardano Summit 2025 and regional tech events" TWGA (India Hackathon component, reported as having met KPIs). The Constitution requires disclosure, not absence, of prior funding; the disclosure obligation is satisfied.

**Article II.7.3 (Net Change Limit Compliance).** A 350,000,000 ada NCL covering epochs 613 to 713 is in force. Consistent with the precedent Ace Alliance established in the Amaru Treasury Withdrawal 2026 rationale and reaffirmed in the Cardano x Draper Dragon Orion Fund rationale, the 350M ada NCL is treated as definitively set and active. The proposal accurately enumerates prior withdrawals against this NCL (Amaru 10,142,000 ada; Dingo 6,900,000 ada; DeFi Liquidity Withdrawal 1 800,000 ada; Orion Fund 50,000,000 ada), leaving a stated remaining balance of 282,158,000 ada. The 3,303,750 ada requested here is well within that headroom.

**Article II.7.4 (Audits and Oversight).** The budget allocates USD 22,421 (3 percent) for legal review, audit provisions, and tax compliance. The proposal additionally commits to a public Intersect dashboard tracking on-chain TRSC and PSSC state, milestone disbursements, and KPI delivery, and to a post-event report within 60 days. These provisions satisfy the requirement that ada be allocated for periodic independent audits and that oversight metrics be implemented.

**Article II.7.5 (Administrator).** The proposal designates Intersect as administrator, operating on behalf of the CDH under a forthcoming legal agreement. Intersect's published administrative structure (three administrative roles, two leadership roles, and a five-member external Oversight Committee composed of Sundae Labs, the Cardano Foundation, DQuadrant, Xerberus, and NMKR) is referenced and is consistent with the administrator-plus-oversight pattern previously accepted by the Constitutional Committee.

**Article II.7.6 (Custody).** Withdrawn ada flows to the Intersect 2025 TRSC at stake address `stake17xzc8pt7fgf0lc0x7eq6z7z6puhsxmzktna7dluahrj6g6ghh5qjr`, with later migration to a 2026 TRSC and downstream funding of a project-specific PSSC for EMURGO. The proposal states explicitly that neither the TRSC nor the PSSCs will be delegated to an SPO; both are delegated to the predefined auto-abstain DRep. The Sundae Labs treasury contract architecture has been audited by TxPipe and MLabs, with a mainnet validation transaction (`0f591dc544ae14102dbb4a74d5311a6acffc1772b163d8b7a9656b9525950b17`) referenced for public verification. Funds remain auditable by the Cardano Community for the duration of administration.

**Appendix I (Guardrails).** TREASURY-01a: the 350M ada NCL was set on-chain by the DReps via the "Net Change Limit (Epoch 613 to Epoch 713)" Info Action, treated as definitively set per our precedent. TREASURY-02a: the 3,303,750 ada requested does not exceed the 350M ada NCL. TREASURY-03a: the withdrawal is denominated in ada.
body.summary: Ace Alliance finds the proposed "Cardano at TOKEN2049 Singapore 2026: Baseline ‘Platinum' Sponsorship Proposal" Treasury Withdrawals Governance Action Constitutional. Rationales are archived at https://github.com/ace-alliance/ace-voting/
